tools.name: accounts.associatePhoneNumberWithUser
tools.description: Associates a phone number with the specified Amazon Chime user.

tools.name: accounts.associateSigninDelegateGroupsWithAccount
tools.description: Associates the specified sign-in delegate groups with the specified Amazon Chime account.

tools.name: accounts.batchCreateRoomMembership
tools.description: Adds up to 50 members to a chat room in an Amazon Chime Enterprise account. Members can be users or bots. The member role designates whether the member is a chat room administrator or a general chat room member.

tools.name: accounts.batchSuspendUser
tools.description:
Suspends up to 50 users from a Team or EnterpriseLWA Amazon Chime account. For more information about different account types, see Managing Your Amazon Chime Accounts in the Amazon Chime Administration Guide.

Users suspended from a Team account are disassociated from the account,but they can continue to use Amazon Chime as free users. To remove the suspension from suspended Team account users, invite them to the Team account again. You can use the InviteUsers action to do so.

Users suspended from an EnterpriseLWA account are immediately signed out of Amazon Chime and can no longer sign in. To remove the suspension from suspended EnterpriseLWA account users, use the BatchUnsuspendUser action.

To sign out users without suspending them, use the LogoutUser action.

tools.name: accounts.batchUnsuspendUser
tools.description:
Removes the suspension from up to 50 previously suspended users for the specified Amazon Chime EnterpriseLWA account. Only users on EnterpriseLWA accounts can be unsuspended using this action. For more information about different account types, see Managing Your Amazon Chime Accounts in the account types, in the Amazon Chime Administration Guide.

Previously suspended users who are unsuspended using this action are returned to Registered status. Users who are not previously suspended are ignored.

tools.name: accounts.batchUpdateUser
tools.description: Updates user details within the UpdateUserRequestItem object for up to 20 users for the specified Amazon Chime account. Currently, only LicenseType updates are supported for this action.

tools.name: accounts.createAccount
tools.description: Creates an Amazon Chime account under the administrator's AWS account. Only Team account types are currently supported for this action. For more information about different account types, see Managing Your Amazon Chime Accounts in the Amazon Chime Administration Guide.

tools.name: accounts.createBot
tools.description: Creates a bot for an Amazon Chime Enterprise account.

tools.name: accounts.createRoom
tools.description: Creates a chat room for the specified Amazon Chime Enterprise account.

tools.name: accounts.createRoomMembership
tools.description: Adds a member to a chat room in an Amazon Chime Enterprise account. A member can be either a user or a bot. The member role designates whether the member is a chat room administrator or a general chat room member.

tools.name: accounts.createUser
tools.description: Creates a user under the specified Amazon Chime account.

tools.name: accounts.deleteAccount
tools.description:
Deletes the specified Amazon Chime account. You must suspend all users before deleting Team account. You can use the BatchSuspendUser action to dodo.

For EnterpriseLWA and EnterpriseAD accounts, you must release the claimed domains for your Amazon Chime account before deletion. As soon as you release the domain, all users under that account are suspended.

Deleted accounts appear in your Disabled accounts list for 90 days. To restore deleted account from your Disabled accounts list, you must contact AWS Support.

After 90 days, deleted accounts are permanently removed from your Disabled accounts list.

tools.name: accounts.deleteEventsConfiguration
tools.description: Deletes the events configuration that allows a bot to receive outgoing events.

tools.name: accounts.deleteRoom
tools.description: Deletes a chat room in an Amazon Chime Enterprise account.

tools.name: accounts.deleteRoomMembership
tools.description: Removes a member from a chat room in an Amazon Chime Enterprise account.

tools.name: accounts.disassociatePhoneNumberFromUser
tools.description: Disassociates the primary provisioned phone number from the specified Amazon Chime user.

tools.name: accounts.disassociateSigninDelegateGroupsFromAccount
tools.description: Disassociates the specified sign-in delegate groups from the specified Amazon Chime account.

tools.name: accounts.getAccount
tools.description: Retrieves details for the specified Amazon Chime account, such as account type and supported licenses.

tools.name: accounts.getAccountSettings
tools.description: Retrieves account settings for the specified Amazon Chime account ID, such as remote control and dialout settings. For more information about these settings, see Use the Policies Page in the Amazon Chime Administration Guide.

tools.name: accounts.getBot
tools.description: Retrieves details for the specified bot, such as bot email address, bot type, status, and display name.

tools.name: accounts.getEventsConfiguration
tools.description: Gets details for an events configuration that allows a bot to receive outgoing events, such as an HTTPS endpoint or Lambda function ARN.

tools.name: accounts.getRetentionSettings
tools.description: Gets the retention settings for the specified Amazon Chime Enterprise account. For more information about retention settings, see Managing Chat Retention Policies in the Amazon Chime Administration Guide.

tools.name: accounts.getRoom
tools.description: Retrieves room details, such as the room name, for a room in an Amazon Chime Enterprise account.

tools.name: accounts.getUser
tools.description:
Retrieves details for the specified user ID, such as primary email address, license type,and personal meeting PIN.

To retrieve user details with an email address instead of a user ID, use the ListUsers action, and then filter by email address.

tools.name: accounts.getUserSettings
tools.description: Retrieves settings for the specified user ID, such as any associated phone number settings.

tools.name: accounts.inviteUsers
tools.description: Sends email to a maximum of 50 users, inviting them to the specified Amazon Chime Team account. Only Team account types are currently supported for this action.

tools.name: accounts.listAccounts
tools.description: Lists the Amazon Chime accounts under the administrator's AWS account. You can filter accounts by account name prefix. To find out which Amazon Chime account a user belongs to, you can filter by the user's email address, which returns one account result.

tools.name: accounts.listBots
tools.description: Lists the bots associated with the administrator's Amazon Chime Enterprise account ID.

tools.name: accounts.listRoomMemberships
tools.description: Lists the membership details for the specified room in an Amazon Chime Enterprise account, such as the members' IDs, email addresses, and names.

tools.name: accounts.listRooms
tools.description: Lists the room details for the specified Amazon Chime Enterprise account. Optionally, filter the results by a member ID (user ID or bot ID) to see a list of rooms that the member belongs to.

tools.name: accounts.listUsers
tools.description: Lists the users that belong to the specified Amazon Chime account. You can specify an email address to list only the user that the email address belongs to.

tools.name: accounts.logoutUser
tools.description: Logs out the specified user from all of the devices they are currently logged into.

tools.name: accounts.putEventsConfiguration
tools.description: Creates an events configuration that allows a bot to receive outgoing events sent by Amazon Chime. Choose either an HTTPS endpoint or a Lambda function ARN. For more information, see Bot.

tools.name: accounts.putRetentionSettings
tools.description:
Puts retention settings for the specified Amazon Chime Enterprise account. We recommend using AWS CloudTrail to monitor usage of this API for your account. For more information, see Logging Amazon Chime API Calls with AWS CloudTrail in the Amazon Chime Administration Guide.

To turn off existing retention settings, remove the number of days from the corresponding RetentionDays field in the RetentionSettings object. For more information about retention settings, see Managing Chat Retention Policies in the Amazon Chime Administration Guide.

tools.name: accounts.redactConversationMessage
tools.description: Redacts the specified message from the specified Amazon Chime conversation.

tools.name: accounts.redactRoomMessage
tools.description: Redacts the specified message from the specified Amazon Chime channel.

tools.name: accounts.regenerateSecurityToken
tools.description: Regenerates the security token for a bot.

tools.name: accounts.resetPersonalPin
tools.description: Resets the personal meeting PIN for the specified user on an Amazon Chime account. Returns the User object with the updated personal meeting PIN.

tools.name: accounts.updateAccount
tools.description: Updates account details for the specified Amazon Chime account. Currently, only account name and default license updates are supported for this action.

tools.name: accounts.updateAccountSettings
tools.description: Updates the settings for the specified Amazon Chime account. You can update settings for remote control of shared screens, or for the dial-out option. For more information about these settings, see Use the Policies Page in the Amazon Chime Administration Guide.

tools.name: accounts.updateBot
tools.description: Updates the status of the specified bot, such as starting or stopping the bot from running in your Amazon Chime Enterprise account.

tools.name: accounts.updateRoom
tools.description: Updates room details, such as the room name, for a room in an Amazon Chime Enterprise account.

tools.name: accounts.updateRoomMembership
tools.description: Updates room membership details, such as the member role, for a room in an Amazon Chime Enterprise account. The member role designates whether the member is a chat room administrator or a general chat room member. The member role can be updated only for user IDs.

tools.name: accounts.updateUser
tools.description: Updates user details for a specified user ID. Currently, only LicenseType updates are supported for this action.

tools.name: accounts.updateUserSettings
tools.description: Updates the settings for the specified user, such as phone number settings.

tools.name: appInstances.createAppInstance
tools.description: Creates an Amazon Chime SDK messaging AppInstance under an AWS account. Only SDK messaging customers use this API. CreateAppInstance supports idempotency behavior as described in the AWS API Standard.

tools.name: appInstances.createAppInstanceAdmin
tools.description:
Promotes an AppInstanceUser to an AppInstanceAdmin. The promoted user can perform the following actions.

  • ChannelModerator actions across all channels in the AppInstance.

  • DeleteChannelMessage actions.

Only an AppInstanceUser can be promoted to an AppInstanceAdmin role.

tools.name: appInstances.deleteAppInstance
tools.description: Deletes an AppInstance and all associated data asynchronously.

tools.name: appInstances.deleteAppInstanceAdmin
tools.description: Demotes an AppInstanceAdmin to an AppInstanceUser. This action does not delete the user.

tools.name: appInstances.deleteAppInstanceStreamingConfigurations
tools.description: Deletes the streaming configurations of an AppInstance.

tools.name: appInstances.describeAppInstance
tools.description: Returns the full details of an AppInstance.

tools.name: appInstances.describeAppInstanceAdmin
tools.description: Returns the full details of an AppInstanceAdmin.

tools.name: appInstances.getAppInstanceRetentionSettings
tools.description: Gets the retention settings for an AppInstance.

tools.name: appInstances.getAppInstanceStreamingConfigurations
tools.description: Gets the streaming settings for an AppInstance.

tools.name: appInstances.listAppInstanceAdmins
tools.description: Returns a list of the administrators in the AppInstance.

tools.name: appInstances.listAppInstances
tools.description: Lists all Amazon Chime AppInstances created under a single AWS account.

tools.name: appInstances.putAppInstanceRetentionSettings
tools.description: Sets the amount of time in days that a given AppInstance retains data.

tools.name: appInstances.putAppInstanceStreamingConfigurations
tools.description: The data streaming configurations of an AppInstance.

tools.name: appInstances.updateAppInstance
tools.description: Updates AppInstance metadata.

tools.name: appInstanceUsers.createAppInstanceUser
tools.description: Creates a user under an Amazon Chime AppInstance. The request consists of a unique appInstanceUserId and Name for that user.

tools.name: appInstanceUsers.deleteAppInstanceUser
tools.description: Deletes an AppInstanceUser.

tools.name: appInstanceUsers.describeAppInstanceUser
tools.description: Returns the full details of an AppInstanceUser.

tools.name: appInstanceUsers.updateAppInstanceUser
tools.description: Updates the details of an AppInstanceUser. You can update names and metadata.

tools.name: appInstanceUsersAppInstanceArn.listAppInstanceUsers
tools.description: List all AppInstanceUsers created under a single AppInstance.

tools.name: channels.batchCreateChannelMembership
tools.description: Adds a specified number of users to a channel.

tools.name: channels.createChannel
tools.description:
Creates a channel to which you can add users and send messages.

Restriction: You can't change a channel's privacy.

The x-amz-chime-bearer request header is mandatory. Use the AppInstanceUserArn of the user that makes the API call as the value in the header.

tools.name: channels.createChannelBan
tools.description:
Permanently bans a member from a channel. Moderators can't add banned members to a channel. To undo a ban, you first have to DeleteChannelBan, and then CreateChannelMembership. Bans are cleaned up when you delete users or channels.

If you ban a user who is already part of a channel, that user is automatically kicked from the channel.

The x-amz-chime-bearer request header is mandatory. Use the AppInstanceUserArn of the user that makes the API call as the value in the header.

tools.name: channels.createChannelMembership
tools.description:
Adds a user to a channel. The InvitedBy response field is derived from the request header. A channel member can:

  • List messages

  • Send messages

  • Receive messages

  • Edit their own messages

  • Leave the channel

Privacy settings impact this action as follows:

  • Public Channels: You do not need to be a member to list messages, but you must be a member to send messages.

  • Private Channels: You must be a member to list or send messages.

The x-amz-chime-bearer request header is mandatory. Use the AppInstanceUserArn of the user that makes the API call as the value in the header.

tools.name: channels.createChannelModerator
tools.description:
Creates a new ChannelModerator. A channel moderator can:

  • Add and remove other members of the channel.

  • Add and remove other moderators of the channel.

  • Add and remove user bans for the channel.

  • Redact messages in the channel.

  • List messages in the channel.

The x-amz-chime-bearer request header is mandatory. Use the AppInstanceUserArn of the user that makes the API call as the value in the header.

tools.name: channels.deleteChannel
tools.description:
Immediately makes a channel and its memberships inaccessible and marks them for deletion. This is an irreversible process.

The x-amz-chime-bearer request header is mandatory. Use the AppInstanceUserArn of the user that makes the API call as the value in the header.

tools.name: channels.deleteChannelBan
tools.description:
Removes a user from a channel's ban list.

The x-amz-chime-bearer request header is mandatory. Use the AppInstanceUserArn of the user that makes the API call as the value in the header.

tools.name: channels.deleteChannelMembership
tools.description:
Removes a member from a channel.

The x-amz-chime-bearer request header is mandatory. Use the AppInstanceUserArn of the user that makes the API call as the value in the header.

tools.name: channels.deleteChannelMessage
tools.description:
Deletes a channel message. Only admins can perform this action. Deletion makes messages inaccessible immediately. A background process deletes any revisions created by UpdateChannelMessage.

The x-amz-chime-bearer request header is mandatory. Use the AppInstanceUserArn of the user that makes the API call as the value in the header.

tools.name: channels.deleteChannelModerator
tools.description:
Deletes a channel moderator.

The x-amz-chime-bearer request header is mandatory. Use the AppInstanceUserArn of the user that makes the API call as the value in the header.

tools.name: channels.describeChannel
tools.description:
Returns the full details of a channel in an Amazon Chime AppInstance.

The x-amz-chime-bearer request header is mandatory. Use the AppInstanceUserArn of the user that makes the API call as the value in the header.

tools.name: channels.describeChannelBan
tools.description:
Returns the full details of a channel ban.

The x-amz-chime-bearer request header is mandatory. Use the AppInstanceUserArn of the user that makes the API call as the value in the header.

tools.name: channels.describeChannelMembership
tools.description:
Returns the full details of a user's channel membership.

The x-amz-chime-bearer request header is mandatory. Use the AppInstanceUserArn of the user that makes the API call as the value in the header.

tools.name: channels.describeChannelMembershipForAppInstanceUser
tools.description:
Returns the details of a channel based on the membership of the specified AppInstanceUser.

The x-amz-chime-bearer request header is mandatory. Use the AppInstanceUserArn of the user that makes the API call as the value in the header.

tools.name: channels.describeChannelModeratedByAppInstanceUser
tools.description:
Returns the full details of a channel moderated by the specified AppInstanceUser.

The x-amz-chime-bearer request header is mandatory. Use the AppInstanceUserArn of the user that makes the API call as the value in the header.

tools.name: channels.describeChannelModerator
tools.description:
Returns the full details of a single ChannelModerator.

The x-amz-chime-bearer request header is mandatory. Use the AppInstanceUserArn of the user that makes the API call as the value in the header.

tools.name: channels.getChannelMessage
tools.description:
Gets the full details of a channel message.

The x-amz-chime-bearer request header is mandatory. Use the AppInstanceUserArn of the user that makes the API call as the value in the header.

tools.name: channels.listChannelBans
tools.description:
Lists all the users banned from a particular channel.

The x-amz-chime-bearer request header is mandatory. Use the AppInstanceUserArn of the user that makes the API call as the value in the header.

tools.name: channels.listChannelMemberships
tools.description:
Lists all channel memberships in a channel.

The x-amz-chime-bearer request header is mandatory. Use the AppInstanceUserArn of the user that makes the API call as the value in the header.

tools.name: channels.listChannelMessages
tools.description:
List all the messages in a channel. Returns a paginated list of ChannelMessages. By default, sorted by creation timestamp in descending order.

Redacted messages appear in the results as empty, since they are only redacted, not deleted. Deleted messages do not appear in the results. This action always returns the latest version of an edited message.

Also, the x-amz-chime-bearer request header is mandatory. Use the AppInstanceUserArn of the user that makes the API call as the value in the header.

tools.name: channels.listChannelModerators
tools.description:
Lists all the moderators for a channel.

The x-amz-chime-bearer request header is mandatory. Use the AppInstanceUserArn of the user that makes the API call as the value in the header.

tools.name: channels.redactChannelMessage
tools.description:
Redacts message content, but not metadata. The message exists in the back end, but the action returns null content, and the state shows as redacted.

The x-amz-chime-bearer request header is mandatory. Use the AppInstanceUserArn of the user that makes the API call as the value in the header.

tools.name: channels.sendChannelMessage
tools.description:
Sends a message to a particular channel that the member is a part of.

The x-amz-chime-bearer request header is mandatory. Use the AppInstanceUserArn of the user that makes the API call as the value in the header.

Also, STANDARD messages can contain 4KB of data and the 1KB of metadata. CONTROL messages can contain 30 bytes of data and no metadata.

tools.name: channels.updateChannel
tools.description:
Update a channel's attributes.

Restriction: You can't change a channel's privacy.

The x-amz-chime-bearer request header is mandatory. Use the AppInstanceUserArn of the user that makes the API call as the value in the header.

tools.name: channels.updateChannelMessage
tools.description:
Updates the content of a message.

The x-amz-chime-bearer request header is mandatory. Use the AppInstanceUserArn of the user that makes the API call as the value in the header.

tools.name: channels.updateChannelReadMarker
tools.description:
The details of the time when a user last read messages in a channel.

The x-amz-chime-bearer request header is mandatory. Use the AppInstanceUserArn of the user that makes the API call as the value in the header.

tools.name: channelsAppInstanceArn.listChannels
tools.description:
Lists all Channels created under a single Chime App as a paginated list. You can specify filters to narrow results.

Functionality & restrictions

  • Use privacy = PUBLIC to retrieve all public channels in the account.

  • Only an AppInstanceAdmin can set privacy = PRIVATE to list the private channels in an account.

The x-amz-chime-bearer request header is mandatory. Use the AppInstanceUserArn of the user that makes the API call as the value in the header.

tools.name: channelsScopeAppInstanceUserMemberships.listChannelMembershipsForAppInstanceUser
tools.description:
Lists all channels that a particular AppInstanceUser is a part of. Only an AppInstanceAdmin can call the API with a user ARN that is not their own.

The x-amz-chime-bearer request header is mandatory. Use the AppInstanceUserArn of the user that makes the API call as the value in the header.

tools.name: channelsScopeAppInstanceUserModeratedChannels.listChannelsModeratedByAppInstanceUser
tools.description:
A list of the channels moderated by an AppInstanceUser.

The x-amz-chime-bearer request header is mandatory. Use the AppInstanceUserArn of the user that makes the API call as the value in the header.

tools.name: emergencyCalling.validateE911Address
tools.description: Validates an address to be used for 911 calls made with Amazon Chime Voice Connectors. You can use validated addresses in a Presence Information Data Format Location Object file that you include in SIP requests. That helps ensure that addresses are routed to the appropriate Public Safety Answering Point.

tools.name: endpoints.getMessagingSessionEndpoint
tools.description: The details of the endpoint for the messaging session.

tools.name: mediaCapturePipelines.createMediaCapturePipeline
tools.description: Creates a media capture pipeline.

tools.name: mediaCapturePipelines.deleteMediaCapturePipeline
tools.description: Deletes the media capture pipeline.

tools.name: mediaCapturePipelines.getMediaCapturePipeline
tools.description: Gets an existing media capture pipeline.

tools.name: mediaCapturePipelines.listMediaCapturePipelines
tools.description: Returns a list of media capture pipelines.

tools.name: meetings.batchCreateAttendee
tools.description: Creates up to 100 new attendees for an active Amazon Chime SDK meeting. For more information about the Amazon Chime SDK, see Using the Amazon Chime SDK in the Amazon Chime SDK Developer Guide.

tools.name: meetings.createAttendee
tools.description: Creates a new attendee for an active Amazon Chime SDK meeting. For more information about the Amazon Chime SDK, see Using the Amazon Chime SDK in the Amazon Chime SDK Developer Guide.

tools.name: meetings.createMeeting
tools.description: Creates a new Amazon Chime SDK meeting in the specified media Region with no initial attendees. For more information about specifying media Regions, see Amazon Chime SDK Media Regions in the Amazon Chime SDK Developer Guide . For more information about the Amazon Chime SDK, see Using the Amazon Chime SDK in the Amazon Chime SDK Developer Guide .

tools.name: meetings.createMeetingDialOut
tools.description:
Uses the join token and call metadata in a meeting request (From number, To number, and so forth) to initiate an outbound call to a public switched telephone network (PSTN) and join them into a Chime meeting. Also ensures that the From number belongs to the customer.

To play welcome audio or implement an interactive voice response (IVR), use the CreateSipMediaApplicationCall action with the corresponding SIP media application ID.

tools.name: meetings.deleteAttendee
tools.description: Deletes an attendee from the specified Amazon Chime SDK meeting and deletes their JoinToken. Attendees are automatically deleted when a Amazon Chime SDK meeting is deleted. For more information about the Amazon Chime SDK, see Using the Amazon Chime SDK in the Amazon Chime SDK Developer Guide.

tools.name: meetings.deleteMeeting
tools.description: Deletes the specified Amazon Chime SDK meeting. The operation deletes all attendees, disconnects all clients, and prevents new clients from joining the meeting. For more information about the Amazon Chime SDK, see Using the Amazon Chime SDK in the Amazon Chime SDK Developer Guide.

tools.name: meetings.getAttendee
tools.description: Gets the Amazon Chime SDK attendee details for a specified meeting ID and attendee ID. For more information about the Amazon Chime SDK, see Using the Amazon Chime SDK in the Amazon Chime SDK Developer Guide .

tools.name: meetings.getMeeting
tools.description: Gets the Amazon Chime SDK meeting details for the specified meeting ID. For more information about the Amazon Chime SDK, see Using the Amazon Chime SDK in the Amazon Chime SDK Developer Guide .

tools.name: meetings.listAttendees
tools.description: Lists the attendees for the specified Amazon Chime SDK meeting. For more information about the Amazon Chime SDK, see Using the Amazon Chime SDK in the Amazon Chime SDK Developer Guide.

tools.name: meetings.listAttendeeTags
tools.description: Lists the tags applied to an Amazon Chime SDK attendee resource.

tools.name: meetings.listMeetings
tools.description: Lists up to 100 active Amazon Chime SDK meetings. For more information about the Amazon Chime SDK, see Using the Amazon Chime SDK in the Amazon Chime SDK Developer Guide.

tools.name: meetings.listMeetingTags
tools.description: Lists the tags applied to an Amazon Chime SDK meeting resource.

tools.name: meetings.startMeetingTranscription
tools.description:
Starts transcription for the specified meetingId. For more information, refer to Using Amazon Chime SDK live transcription in the Amazon Chime SDK Developer Guide.

If you specify an invalid configuration, a TranscriptFailed event will be sent with the contents of the BadRequestException generated by Amazon Transcribe. For more information on each parameter and which combinations are valid, refer to the StartStreamTranscription API in the Amazon Transcribe Developer Guide.

Amazon Chime SDK live transcription is powered by Amazon Transcribe. Use of Amazon Transcribe is subject to the AWS Service Terms, including the terms specific to the AWS Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence Services.

tools.name: meetings.stopMeetingTranscription
tools.description: Stops transcription for the specified meetingId.

tools.name: meetings.tagAttendee
tools.description: Applies the specified tags to the specified Amazon Chime SDK attendee.

tools.name: meetings.tagMeeting
tools.description: Applies the specified tags to the specified Amazon Chime SDK meeting.

tools.name: meetings.untagAttendee
tools.description: Untags the specified tags from the specified Amazon Chime SDK attendee.

tools.name: meetings.untagMeeting
tools.description: Untags the specified tags from the specified Amazon Chime SDK meeting.

tools.name: meetingsOperationCreateAttendees.createMeetingWithAttendees
tools.description: Creates a new Amazon Chime SDK meeting in the specified media Region, with attendees. For more information about specifying media Regions, see Amazon Chime SDK Media Regions in the Amazon Chime SDK Developer Guide . For more information about the Amazon Chime SDK, see Using the Amazon Chime SDK in the Amazon Chime SDK Developer Guide .

tools.name: phoneNumberCountriesProductType.listSupportedPhoneNumberCountries
tools.description: Lists supported phone number countries.

tools.name: phoneNumberOrders.createPhoneNumberOrder
tools.description: Creates an order for phone numbers to be provisioned. For toll-free numbers, you cannot use the Amazon Chime Business Calling product type. For numbers outside the U.S., you must use the Amazon Chime SIP Media Application Dial-In product type.

tools.name: phoneNumberOrders.getPhoneNumberOrder
tools.description: Retrieves details for the specified phone number order, such as the order creation timestamp, phone numbers in E.164 format, product type, and order status.

tools.name: phoneNumberOrders.listPhoneNumberOrders
tools.description: Lists the phone number orders for the administrator's Amazon Chime account.

tools.name: phoneNumbers.deletePhoneNumber
tools.description:
Moves the specified phone number into the Deletion queue. A phone number must be disassociated from any users or Amazon Chime Voice Connectors before it can be deleted.

Deleted phone numbers remain in the Deletion queue for 7 days before they are deleted permanently.

tools.name: phoneNumbers.getPhoneNumber
tools.description: Retrieves details for the specified phone number ID, such as associations, capabilities, and product type.

tools.name: phoneNumbers.listPhoneNumbers
tools.description: Lists the phone numbers for the specified Amazon Chime account, Amazon Chime user, Amazon Chime Voice Connector, or Amazon Chime Voice Connector group.

tools.name: phoneNumbers.restorePhoneNumber
tools.description: Moves a phone number from the Deletion queue back into the phone number Inventory.

tools.name: phoneNumbers.updatePhoneNumber
tools.description:
Updates phone number details, such as product type or calling name, for the specified phone number ID. You can update one phone number detail at a time. For example, you can update either the product type or the calling name in one action.

For toll-free numbers, you cannot use the Amazon Chime Business Calling product type. For numbers outside the U.S., you must use the Amazon Chime SIP Media Application Dial-In product type.

Updates to outbound calling names can take 72 hours to complete. Pending updates to outbound calling names must be complete before you can request another update.

tools.name: phoneNumbersOperationBatchDelete.batchDeletePhoneNumber
tools.description:
Moves phone numbers into the Deletion queue. Phone numbers must be disassociated from any users or Amazon Chime Voice Connectors before they can be deleted.

Phone numbers remain in the Deletion queue for 7 days before they are deleted permanently.

tools.name: phoneNumbersOperationBatchUpdate.batchUpdatePhoneNumber
tools.description:
Updates phone number product types or calling names. You can update one attribute at a time for each UpdatePhoneNumberRequestItem. For example, you can update the product type or the calling name.

For toll-free numbers, you cannot use the Amazon Chime Business Calling product type. For numbers outside the U.S., you must use the Amazon Chime SIP Media Application Dial-In product type.

Updates to outbound calling names can take up to 72 hours to complete. Pending updates to outbound calling names must be complete before you can request another update.

tools.name: searchTypePhoneNumbers.searchAvailablePhoneNumbers
tools.description: Searches for phone numbers that can be ordered. For US numbers, provide at least one of the following search filters: AreaCode, City, State, or TollFreePrefix. If you provide City, you must also provide State. Numbers outside the US only support the PhoneNumberType filter, which you must use.

tools.name: settings.getGlobalSettings
tools.description: Retrieves global settings for the administrator's AWS account, such as Amazon Chime Business Calling and Amazon Chime Voice Connector settings.

tools.name: settings.getPhoneNumberSettings
tools.description: Retrieves the phone number settings for the administrator's AWS account, such as the default outbound calling name.

tools.name: settings.updateGlobalSettings
tools.description: Updates global settings for the administrator's AWS account, such as Amazon Chime Business Calling and Amazon Chime Voice Connector settings.

tools.name: settings.updatePhoneNumberSettings
tools.description: Updates the phone number settings for the administrator's AWS account, such as the default outbound calling name. You can update the default outbound calling name once every seven days. Outbound calling names can take up to 72 hours to update.

tools.name: sipMediaApplications.createSipMediaApplication
tools.description: Creates a SIP media application.

tools.name: sipMediaApplications.createSipMediaApplicationCall
tools.description: Creates an outbound call to a phone number from the phone number specified in the request, and it invokes the endpoint of the specified sipMediaApplicationId.

tools.name: sipMediaApplications.deleteSipMediaApplication
tools.description: Deletes a SIP media application.

tools.name: sipMediaApplications.getSipMediaApplication
tools.description: Retrieves the information for a SIP media application, including name, AWS Region, and endpoints.

tools.name: sipMediaApplications.getSipMediaApplicationLoggingConfiguration
tools.description: Returns the logging configuration for the specified SIP media application.

tools.name: sipMediaApplications.listSipMediaApplications
tools.description: Lists the SIP media applications under the administrator's AWS account.

tools.name: sipMediaApplications.putSipMediaApplicationLoggingConfiguration
tools.description: Updates the logging configuration for the specified SIP media application.

tools.name: sipMediaApplications.updateSipMediaApplication
tools.description: Updates the details of the specified SIP media application.

tools.name: sipMediaApplications.updateSipMediaApplicationCall
tools.description: Invokes the AWS Lambda function associated with the SIP media application and transaction ID in an update request. The Lambda function can then return a new set of actions.

tools.name: sipRules.createSipRule
tools.description: Creates a SIP rule which can be used to run a SIP media application as a target for a specific trigger type.

tools.name: sipRules.deleteSipRule
tools.description: Deletes a SIP rule. You must disable a SIP rule before you can delete it.

tools.name: sipRules.getSipRule
tools.description: Retrieves the details of a SIP rule, such as the rule ID, name, triggers, and target endpoints.

tools.name: sipRules.listSipRules
tools.description: Lists the SIP rules under the administrator's AWS account.

tools.name: sipRules.updateSipRule
tools.description: Updates the details of the specified SIP rule.

tools.name: tagsArn.listTagsForResource
tools.description: Lists the tags applied to an Amazon Chime SDK meeting resource.

tools.name: tagsOperationTagResource.tagResource
tools.description: Applies the specified tags to the specified Amazon Chime SDK meeting resource.

tools.name: tagsOperationUntagResource.untagResource
tools.description: Untags the specified tags from the specified Amazon Chime SDK meeting resource.

tools.name: voiceConnectorGroups.associatePhoneNumbersWithVoiceConnectorGroup
tools.description: Associates phone numbers with the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector group.

tools.name: voiceConnectorGroups.createVoiceConnectorGroup
tools.description:
Creates an Amazon Chime Voice Connector group under the administrator's AWS account. You can associate Amazon Chime Voice Connectors with the Amazon Chime Voice Connector group by including VoiceConnectorItems in the request.

You can include Amazon Chime Voice Connectors from different AWS Regions in your group. This creates a fault tolerant mechanism for fallback in case of availability events.

tools.name: voiceConnectorGroups.deleteVoiceConnectorGroup
tools.description: Deletes the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector group. Any VoiceConnectorItems and phone numbers associated with the group must be removed before it can be deleted.

tools.name: voiceConnectorGroups.disassociatePhoneNumbersFromVoiceConnectorGroup
tools.description: Disassociates the specified phone numbers from the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector group.

tools.name: voiceConnectorGroups.getVoiceConnectorGroup
tools.description: Retrieves details for the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector group, such as timestamps,name, and associated VoiceConnectorItems.

tools.name: voiceConnectorGroups.listVoiceConnectorGroups
tools.description: Lists the Amazon Chime Voice Connector groups for the administrator's AWS account.

tools.name: voiceConnectorGroups.updateVoiceConnectorGroup
tools.description: Updates details of the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector group, such as the name and Amazon Chime Voice Connector priority ranking.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.associatePhoneNumbersWithVoiceConnector
tools.description: Associates phone numbers with the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.createProxySession
tools.description: Creates a proxy session on the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector for the specified participant phone numbers.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.createVoiceConnector
tools.description:
Creates an Amazon Chime Voice Connector under the administrator's AWS account. You can choose to create an Amazon Chime Voice Connector in a specific AWS Region.

Enabling CreateVoiceConnectorRequest$RequireEncryption configures your Amazon Chime Voice Connector to use TLS transport for SIP signaling and Secure RTP (SRTP) for media. Inbound calls use TLS transport, and unencrypted outbound calls are blocked.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.deleteProxySession
tools.description: Deletes the specified proxy session from the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.deleteVoiceConnector
tools.description: Deletes the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector. Any phone numbers associated with the Amazon Chime Voice Connector must be disassociated from it before it can be deleted.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.deleteVoiceConnectorEmergencyCallingConfiguration
tools.description: Deletes the emergency calling configuration details from the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.deleteVoiceConnectorOrigination
tools.description:
Deletes the origination settings for the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector.

If emergency calling is configured for the Amazon Chime Voice Connector, it must be deleted prior to deleting the origination settings.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.deleteVoiceConnectorProxy
tools.description: Deletes the proxy configuration from the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.deleteVoiceConnectorStreamingConfiguration
tools.description: Deletes the streaming configuration for the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.deleteVoiceConnectorTermination
tools.description:
Deletes the termination settings for the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector.

If emergency calling is configured for the Amazon Chime Voice Connector, it must be deleted prior to deleting the termination settings.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.deleteVoiceConnectorTerminationCredentials
tools.description: Deletes the specified SIP credentials used by your equipment to authenticate during call termination.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.disassociatePhoneNumbersFromVoiceConnector
tools.description: Disassociates the specified phone numbers from the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.getProxySession
tools.description: Gets the specified proxy session details for the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.getVoiceConnector
tools.description: Retrieves details for the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector, such as timestamps,name, outbound host, and encryption requirements.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.getVoiceConnectorEmergencyCallingConfiguration
tools.description: Gets the emergency calling configuration details for the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.getVoiceConnectorLoggingConfiguration
tools.description: Retrieves the logging configuration details for the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector. Shows whether SIP message logs are enabled for sending to Amazon CloudWatch Logs.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.getVoiceConnectorOrigination
tools.description: Retrieves origination setting details for the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.getVoiceConnectorProxy
tools.description: Gets the proxy configuration details for the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.getVoiceConnectorStreamingConfiguration
tools.description: Retrieves the streaming configuration details for the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector. Shows whether media streaming is enabled for sending to Amazon Kinesis. It also shows the retention period, in hours, for the Amazon Kinesis data.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.getVoiceConnectorTermination
tools.description: Retrieves termination setting details for the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.getVoiceConnectorTerminationHealth
tools.description: Retrieves information about the last time a SIP OPTIONS ping was received from your SIP infrastructure for the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.listProxySessions
tools.description: Lists the proxy sessions for the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.listVoiceConnectors
tools.description: Lists the Amazon Chime Voice Connectors for the administrator's AWS account.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.listVoiceConnectorTerminationCredentials
tools.description: Lists the SIP credentials for the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.putVoiceConnectorEmergencyCallingConfiguration
tools.description: Puts emergency calling configuration details to the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector, such as emergency phone numbers and calling countries. Origination and termination settings must be enabled for the Amazon Chime Voice Connector before emergency calling can be configured.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.putVoiceConnectorLoggingConfiguration
tools.description: Adds a logging configuration for the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector. The logging configuration specifies whether SIP message logs are enabled for sending to Amazon CloudWatch Logs.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.putVoiceConnectorOrigination
tools.description:
Adds origination settings for the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector.

If emergency calling is configured for the Amazon Chime Voice Connector, it must be deleted prior to turning off origination settings.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.putVoiceConnectorProxy
tools.description: Puts the specified proxy configuration to the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.putVoiceConnectorStreamingConfiguration
tools.description: Adds a streaming configuration for the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector. The streaming configuration specifies whether media streaming is enabled for sending to Kinesis. It also sets the retention period, in hours, for the Amazon Kinesis data.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.putVoiceConnectorTermination
tools.description:
Adds termination settings for the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector.

If emergency calling is configured for the Amazon Chime Voice Connector, it must be deleted prior to turning off termination settings.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.putVoiceConnectorTerminationCredentials
tools.description: Adds termination SIP credentials for the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.updateProxySession
tools.description: Updates the specified proxy session details, such as voice or SMS capabilities.

tools.name: voiceConnectors.updateVoiceConnector
tools.description: Updates details for the specified Amazon Chime Voice Connector.

tools.name: openapi.previewSpec
tools.description: Preview an OpenAPI document before adding it as a source

tools.name: openapi.addSource
tools.description: Add an OpenAPI source and register its operations as tools
